summary: Gruber reviews two new products from Clicks, the startup that previously made BlackBerry-style keyboard cases for iPhones. The first is the Clicks Communicator, a full Android phone with a hardware keyboard pitched as a messaging-focused second device for $500. The second is the Clicks Power Keyboard, a MagSafe battery pack with a slide-out Bluetooth keyboard that works with any modern phone, iPad, Apple TV, or Vision Pro for $110. Gruber admits he never took to the original keyboard case but finds the Power Keyboard's device-agnostic MagSafe approach a much smarter product design. He praises the team's ambition in simultaneously launching a phone and a universal keyboard accessory just two years after their modest first product.
key_points: Gruber's experience with the original Clicks keyboard case was underwhelming — he typed much slower than with the on-screen keyboard and found the form factor unwieldy
The Clicks Power Keyboard solves the biggest problem with the case approach: MagSafe and Bluetooth make it universal across devices instead of requiring per-model cases
The Clicks Communicator is a full BlackBerry-style Android phone focused on messaging, pitched as a less distracting second device at $500
Gruber highlights the strategic advantage of device-agnostic accessories over model-specific cases, noting Clicks still lacks cases for the latest iPhones
The team's ambition in going from a niche accessory to launching their own phone earns Gruber's admiration
money_quotes: I wound up typing slower — much slower — with my Clicks keyboard case than I did using the on-screen keyboard.
I have zero need for one but I kind of want one.
This strikes me as a much better idea for a hardware phone keyboard accessory than a case.
Cases need to be made per each device. A Clicks keyboard case for an iPhone 15 won't fit an iPhone 16. Hell, a case for an iPhone 15 won't even fit an iPhone 15 Pro.
MagSafe and Bluetooth mean the same Clicks Power Keyboard will work with any modern iPhone — or Android phone.
It comes in one color, black (the correct color if you're only going to offer one).
I just love the chutzpah of these guys.

They started with a good minimal launch product and are back two years later with what looks like a much better idea for a phone keyboard accessory. But they're also now making their own whole goddamn phone. That's going big, not going home.
key_insight: A universal MagSafe/Bluetooth keyboard accessory is a fundamentally better product concept than model-specific keyboard cases, because it decouples the accessory from the relentless annual phone upgrade cycle.
